About Iyana

A tech-driven leader built for her city.

Iyana M. Farrar is a Charlotte native and emerging business and technology professional with a degree in Management Information Systems and an economics minor. She is an Operations & Project Management Consultant specializing in building structured systems and helping businesses operate with intention and efficiency.

As a Project Management and Marketing Intern with University City Partners, Iyana co-created the University City Food Truck Park Proposal — a full-scope community development project covering market research, monetization strategy, economic analysis, risk management, and a final pitch deck.

With a hands-on approach and a strategic eye, Iyana partners with clients to turn operational challenges into clear, scalable solutions — from systems design to full-scale strategy setup.

“I care deeply about the spaces I enter and the people within them. Whether in professional settings, classrooms, or community initiatives, I strive to add value through collaboration, development, and genuine connection.”

— Iyana M. Farrar

Services designed to create lasting operational value.

Featured Work • 2025–2026

University City
Food Truck Park

Co-created as part of Iyana’s internship at University City Partners, the Food Truck Park Proposal was not just a project — it was a vision for what University City could become as a cultural and culinary destination. The concept activates underused land to support small business incubation and community placemaking near 7605 University City Blvd.

University City Food Truck Park

Near UNC Charlotte • Strategic placemaking • Small business incubation • 28–30 hrs/week Year 1

Scope of Work

Market research • Monetization strategies • Economic development analysis • Risk management • Capital campaign structure • Pitch deck • Site renderings • One-pagers

Community Impact

Designed as a launchpad for small businesses, creating a pipeline to brick-and-mortar establishments while fostering residency, business development, and lasting partnerships within University City.

Recognition

Keith Stanley, President of University City Partners, noted Iyana’s ability to see the concept not simply as an event activation, but as a strategic tool for placemaking and small business incubation.

The Proposal

From vision to framework

University City Partners • 2025
“

She asked thoughtful questions, offered creative insights, and approached the project with a strong sense of ownership. She represents the type of emerging leader who understands how community focused ideas must be paired with disciplined execution.

Keith Stanley
President, University City Partners • February 2026

What We Stand For

Three words that define the work.

01

Determination

Every project, every deliverable, every community initiative is approached with unwavering drive. Planning, organization, and consistent follow-through are non-negotiable standards at Farrar Operations Group.

02

Resilience

“My journey has not been easy, but it has shaped me into someone who believes deeply in consistency, accountability, and perseverance.” Resilience isn’t a buzzword here — it’s lived experience brought to every engagement.

03

Ambition

From sports industry innovation to tech-driven business leadership, the vision is long-term and expansive. Ambition here is paired with humility and a deep commitment to being a positive example for the next generation.

More Than a Consultant

Grounded in purpose.

Outside of her professional work, Iyana prioritizes health and wellness, writing, and creative expression as the practices that keep her grounded and balanced. She is a natural problem solver with a strong creative drive who values continuous learning at every stage of life.

She is dedicated to being a positive example for young people and aspiring professionals navigating uncertain or challenging paths — because she has been there, and she came through it with her values intact.

“Yes, the journey can be challenging — but one can accomplish anything by being determined, consistent, and accountable.”
